name: Hebrew alphabet (alef-bet)
description: The 22 letters of the Hebrew abjad (consonants; vowels are added as points). Written right-to-left. Five letters take a special final form. Each letter is tested both ways, tracked separately.
primer:
The Hebrew alphabet (**alef-bet**) is an **abjad** of **22 consonant letters** written **right-to-left**. Vowels are not letters — they are added below/beside consonants as points (*niqqud*), usually omitted in everyday text. **Five letters take a distinct *final* form** (sofit) when they end a word: kaf, mem, nun, pe, tsadi.

**Full mapping — letter → name → sound:**

- **א** — alef — silent / glottal stop (carries a vowel)
- **ב** — bet — b (or 'v' without the dot)
- **ג** — gimel — g (hard)
- **ד** — dalet — d
- **ה** — he — h
- **ו** — vav — v (or o / u as a vowel)
- **ז** — zayin — z
- **ח** — chet — kh (guttural, as in 'loch')
- **ט** — tet — t
- **י** — yod — y
- **כ** — kaf — k (or 'kh'); **final form ך**
- **ל** — lamed — l
- **מ** — mem — m; **final form ם**
- **נ** — nun — n; **final form ן**
- **ס** — samekh — s
- **ע** — ayin — silent / guttural
- **פ** — pe — p (or 'f'); **final form ף**
- **צ** — tsadi — ts; **final form ץ**
- **ק** — qof — k
- **ר** — resh — r
- **ש** — shin — sh (or 's')
- **ת** — tav — t

**Special cases:** the five final forms are **ך ם ן ף ץ** (kaf, mem, nun, pe, tsadi); א (alef) and ע (ayin) are silent carriers; a dot (*dagesh*) hardens some letters (ב b vs v, פ p vs f); ש takes a dot to show shin 'sh' vs sin 's'.

cards.definition: alef — silent / glottal stop (carries a vowel)
vav — v (or o / u as a vowel)

cards.note: Takes a final form ך at the end of a word.
Takes a final form ם at the end of a word.
Takes a final form ן at the end of a word.
Takes a final form ף at the end of a word.
Takes a final form ץ at the end of a word.
